raw_responses.query: arxiv 2025 LLM agent consistency self-contradiction prior claim tracking

results.title: Evaluation and Benchmarking of LLM Agents: A Survey
results.snippet: Memory-enhanced evaluation techniques track how well agents maintain consistency in long-horizon tasks, with metrics including Consistency Score (no contradictions between turns). In ReAct-style agents, state is tracked implicitly within natural language outputs making it prone to drift and contradiction; reasoning appears locally coherent while becoming globally inconsistent over longer horizons.

results.title: Contradiction to Consensus: Dual-Perspective, Multi-Source Retrieval-Based Claim Verification with Source-Level Disagreement using LLM
results.snippet: Claim verification framework using dual-perspective multi-source retrieval with source-level disagreement detection by LLM.

results.title: Model-First Reasoning LLM Agents: Reducing Hallucinations through Explicit Problem Modeling
results.snippet: Reducing hallucinations in LLM agents through explicit problem modeling.

results.title: Memory for Autonomous LLM Agents: Mechanisms, Evaluation, and Emerging Frontiers
results.snippet: Agent memory evaluation must address concerns including staleness, contradiction, forgetting quality, and governance compliance.

results.title: LLM-based Agents Suffer from Hallucinations: A Survey of Taxonomy, Methods, and Directions
results.snippet: Survey of hallucination taxonomy and methods in LLM-based agents.

results.title: MAR: Multi-Agent Reflexion Improves Reasoning Abilities in LLMs
results.snippet: Multi-agent reflexion; Reflexion frameworks are vulnerable to degeneration-of-thought, where the agent repeats the same flawed reasoning even when failures are identified.

results.title: A Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents
results.snippet: Survey covering self-reflection, self-consistency, majority voting, confidence-weighted schemes for LLM agents.

results.title: AgentHER: Hindsight Experience Replay for LLM Agent Trajectory Relabeling
results.snippet: Hindsight experience replay applied to LLM agent trajectories for relabeling.

raw_responses.query: arxiv 2025 LLM agent commitment reliance dependency graph claim retraction

results.title: AGENTARMOR: Enforcing Program Analysis on Agent Runtime Trace to Defend
results.snippet: AgentArmor transforms unstructured agent runtime messages into Program Dependence Graphs (PDGs) using a dependency analyzer that infers structured data and control dependencies from natural language. The root cause of agent vulnerabilities lies in the untraceable dependencies of their execution.

results.title: Reliable agent engineering should integrate machine-compatible organizational principles
results.snippet: Discusses commitment to resilience for LLM agents, requiring developing capabilities to detect, contain, and recover from errors. Parallels between LLM agents and organization science.

results.title: Securing Large Language Model Agents via Structured Graph Abstraction
results.snippet: Structured graph abstraction for securing LLM agents; dependency analysis over runtime traces.

results.title: Beyond Task Completion: An Assessment Framework for Evaluating Agentic AI Systems
results.snippet: Assessment framework evaluating agentic AI systems beyond end-task success.

results.title: Externalization in LLM Agents: A Unified Review of Memory, Skills, Protocols and Harness Engineering
results.snippet: Unified review of externalization in LLM agents covering memory, skills, protocols, and harness engineering.

results.title: Graph-Augmented Large Language Model Agents: Current Progress and Future Prospects
results.snippet: Survey of representative methods that model agentic workflows as graphs where nodes represent LLM invocations and edges capture logical dependencies and execution flow.

results.title: Large Language Models in Legal Systems: A Survey
results.snippet: Survey of LLM applications in legal systems.

results.title: Principle II.4 - Agency by estoppel / apparent authority
results.snippet: Agency by estoppel and apparent authority principle in commercial law. Estoppel ensures consistency in legal interpretations, preventing parties from contradicting prior statements or actions.

results.title: What is Estoppel? — Kreo Glossary
results.snippet: Estoppel is the legal doctrine that prevents a person from contradicting their own previous statements, representations, or behaviors if such contradictions would unfairly affect another party who relied on the original representations.

results.title: Legal aspects of generative artificial intelligence and large language models in examinations and theses
results.snippet: Legal aspects of generative AI and LLMs in academic examinations.

results.snippet: Stanford working paper on whether LLMs should be subject to fiduciary duties.

results.title: Estoppel | Law, Types, and Key Applications
results.snippet: Estoppel types include promissory estoppel, equitable estoppel, judicial estoppel, and collateral estoppel/issue preclusion.

results.title: Challenges for Generative AI in Legal Reasoning
results.snippet: Challenges for generative AI in legal reasoning including selecting the correct legal framework, generating sound arguments based on doctrine of sources of law, distinguishing ratio decidendi and obiter dicta, resolving ambiguity, managing conflicting legal provisions, and applying burden of proof.
